summary: Average length of stay for adult families in shelter has fallen by more than half since FY22, dropping every year through FY25. DHS attributes the decline both to a large share of recently arrived asylum-seeking families with inherently shorter stays and, more recently, a deliberate agency push in FY25 to speed exits for long-term shelter stayers in this population.
oneLiner: decreasing due to shorter stays among new arrivals and a deliberate push to speed exits
